summary: Raises an activity warning when one fiat transaction crosses the client review threshold.
Fixed platform threshold for large individual transaction review.
Raises a one-time warning when accumulated client volume crosses the lower review threshold.
Raises a one-time warning when accumulated client volume crosses the high review threshold.
Raises a warning when a client creates three transactions inside a 24 hour window.
Represents wallet reputation alerts returned by the external KYCAID risk check.
kycaidRiskReason: Represents wallet reputation alerts returned by the external KYCAID risk check.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Represents wallet reputation alerts returned by the external KYCAID risk check.

summary: Trigger: Customer, fiat sender/beneficiary, wallet address, blockchain cluster, or counterparty matches sanctions screening. Applies to: Fiat and crypto transactions. Review: Confirm match quality, identifiers, wallet ownership, transaction status, and whether the transaction must be rejected, frozen, or escalated.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer, fiat sender/beneficiary, wallet address, blockchain cluster, or counterparty matches sanctions screening. Applies to: Fiat and crypto transactions. Review: Confirm match quality, identifiers, wallet ownership, transaction status, and whether the transaction must be rejected, frozen, or escalated.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer, fiat sender/beneficiary, wallet address, blockchain cluster, or counterparty matches sanctions screening. Applies to: Fiat and crypto transactions. Review: Confirm match quality, identifiers, wallet ownership, transaction status, and whether the transaction must be rejected, frozen, or escalated.

summary: Trigger: Requested payout wallet or incoming crypto wallet has exposure to darknet markets, scams, ransomware, stolen funds, sanctioned entities, terrorist financing, or other high-risk services. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check direct/indirect exposure, exposure percentage, hop distance, transaction amount, and customer explanation.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Requested payout wallet or incoming crypto wallet has exposure to darknet markets, scams, ransomware, stolen funds, sanctioned entities, terrorist financing, or other high-risk services. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check direct/indirect exposure, exposure percentage, hop distance, transaction amount, and customer explanation.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Requested payout wallet or incoming crypto wallet has exposure to darknet markets, scams, ransomware, stolen funds, sanctioned entities, terrorist financing, or other high-risk services. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check direct/indirect exposure, exposure percentage, hop distance, transaction amount, and customer explanation.

summary: Trigger: Customer requests crypto to be sent to, or sends crypto from, a wallet linked to mixers, tumblers, coinjoin services, peeling chains, or other obfuscation methods. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Identify whether exposure is direct or indirect and whether the customer has repeated similar activity.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer requests crypto to be sent to, or sends crypto from, a wallet linked to mixers, tumblers, coinjoin services, peeling chains, or other obfuscation methods. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Identify whether exposure is direct or indirect and whether the customer has repeated similar activity.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer requests crypto to be sent to, or sends crypto from, a wallet linked to mixers, tumblers, coinjoin services, peeling chains, or other obfuscation methods. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Identify whether exposure is direct or indirect and whether the customer has repeated similar activity.

summary: Trigger: Fiat payment comes from a bank account, card, or payment method not held in the verified customer's name. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Compare sender name with KYC name. Request proof of relationship or reject if third-party funding is prohibited.

Trigger: Customer requests fiat payout to a bank account not held in their own verified name. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check beneficiary ownership, account name, country, and whether payout violates internal policy.

Trigger: Two or more unrelated customers use the same IBAN, card, payment account, or fiat sender/beneficiary details. Applies to: Fiat deposits and payouts. Review: Check shared address, device, IP, wallet address, and possible mule network activity.

Trigger: Multiple unrelated customers request crypto to the same wallet address or send crypto from the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check customer links, common fiat accounts, shared IP/device, and wallet cluster.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Multiple unrelated customers request crypto to the same wallet address or send crypto from the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check customer links, common fiat accounts, shared IP/device, and wallet cluster.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Multiple unrelated customers request crypto to the same wallet address or send crypto from the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check customer links, common fiat accounts, shared IP/device, and wallet cluster.

summary: Trigger: A customer uses several fiat payment methods but repeatedly requests crypto delivery to the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check ownership of all payment methods and whether the wallet appears to collect funds from multiple people.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: A customer uses several fiat payment methods but repeatedly requests crypto delivery to the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check ownership of all payment methods and whether the wallet appears to collect funds from multiple people.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: A customer uses several fiat payment methods but repeatedly requests crypto delivery to the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check ownership of all payment methods and whether the wallet appears to collect funds from multiple people.

summary: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from several unrelated wallets and requests fiat payout to the same bank account. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check wallet ownership, source of funds, and whether the customer is acting for third parties.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from several unrelated wallets and requests fiat payout to the same bank account. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check wallet ownership, source of funds, and whether the customer is acting for third parties.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from several unrelated wallets and requests fiat payout to the same bank account. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check wallet ownership, source of funds, and whether the customer is acting for third parties.

summary: Trigger: Customer performs several transactions just below internal review thresholds. Applies to: Fiat deposits, fiat payouts, crypto purchases, and crypto sales. Example: Three or more transactions just below EUR 1,000 / EUR 3,000 / EUR 10,000 within 24 hours or 7 days. Review: Check whether the pattern appears designed to avoid checks.

Trigger: Newly onboarded customer performs a high-value transaction shortly after registration. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Example: Transaction above EUR 5,000 within 7 days of onboarding. Review: Check KYC quality, source of funds, wallet risk, payment ownership, and IP/device risk.

Trigger: Transaction amount or frequency is inconsistent with customer occupation, income, expected activity, or previous behavior. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Request source of funds/source of wealth where needed.

Trigger: Customer performs unusually frequent transactions in a short period. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Example: More than 5 transactions in 24 hours or 10 transactions in 7 days, depending on normal customer behavior. Review: Check whether activity is normal market behavior, business activity, or possible layering.

Trigger: Customer repeatedly changes requested crypto payout wallet addresses. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check whether wallets are connected, high-risk, newly created, or linked to suspicious clusters.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer repeatedly changes requested crypto payout wallet addresses. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check whether wallets are connected, high-risk, newly created, or linked to suspicious clusters.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer repeatedly changes requested crypto payout wallet addresses. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check whether wallets are connected, high-risk, newly created, or linked to suspicious clusters.

summary: Trigger: Customer, fiat bank, IP address, wallet service, or counterparty is linked to a high-risk or restricted jurisdiction. Applies to: Fiat and crypto transactions. Review: Check country risk, customer residency, payment country, IP location, and sanctions restrictions.

Trigger: Customer initiates transaction using VPN/proxy/TOR or from a country inconsistent with KYC/residency. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Check login history, device changes, travel explanation, and whether the country is restricted.

Trigger: Customer sends crypto from a wallet with high-risk blockchain exposure and requests fiat payout. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Review source wallet exposure, incoming transaction history, asset type, and customer explanation.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from a wallet with high-risk blockchain exposure and requests fiat payout. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Review source wallet exposure, incoming transaction history, asset type, and customer explanation.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from a wallet with high-risk blockchain exposure and requests fiat payout. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Review source wallet exposure, incoming transaction history, asset type, and customer explanation.

summary: Trigger: Customer pays fiat and requests crypto delivery to a wallet with high-risk indicators. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: This is the corrected version of the earlier rule. The alert is not because the customer buys crypto, but because the requested destination wallet is risky.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer pays fiat and requests crypto delivery to a wallet with high-risk indicators. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: This is the corrected version of the earlier rule. The alert is not because the customer buys crypto, but because the requested destination wallet is risky.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer pays fiat and requests crypto delivery to a wallet with high-risk indicators. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: This is the corrected version of the earlier rule. The alert is not because the customer buys crypto, but because the requested destination wallet is risky.

summary: Trigger: Customer uses privacy coins or assets/chains with reduced traceability, where supported or detected. Applies to: Crypto transactions. Review: Check customer purpose, jurisdiction, amount, and repeated use.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer uses privacy coins or assets/chains with reduced traceability, where supported or detected. Applies to: Crypto transactions. Review: Check customer purpose, jurisdiction, amount, and repeated use.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer uses privacy coins or assets/chains with reduced traceability, where supported or detected. Applies to: Crypto transactions. Review: Check customer purpose, jurisdiction, amount, and repeated use.

summary: Trigger: Customer activity involves rapid conversion between assets/chains before or after using the exchange. Applies to: Mainly crypto-to-fiat, but also relevant for fiat-to-crypto wallet review. Review: Look for bridge use, rapid swaps, obfuscation patterns, and high-risk end wallets.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer activity involves rapid conversion between assets/chains before or after using the exchange. Applies to: Mainly crypto-to-fiat, but also relevant for fiat-to-crypto wallet review. Review: Look for bridge use, rapid swaps, obfuscation patterns, and high-risk end wallets.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer activity involves rapid conversion between assets/chains before or after using the exchange. Applies to: Mainly crypto-to-fiat, but also relevant for fiat-to-crypto wallet review. Review: Look for bridge use, rapid swaps, obfuscation patterns, and high-risk end wallets.

summary: Trigger: Incoming or requested wallet is linked to cross-chain bridges, especially combined with high-risk exposure. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Review source chain, destination chain, bridge risk, and wallet path.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Incoming or requested wallet is linked to cross-chain bridges, especially combined with high-risk exposure. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Review source chain, destination chain, bridge risk, and wallet path.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Incoming or requested wallet is linked to cross-chain bridges, especially combined with high-risk exposure. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Review source chain, destination chain, bridge risk, and wallet path.

summary: Trigger: Required originator/beneficiary information is missing, incomplete, inconsistent, or rejected by counterparty CASP/VASP. Applies to: Crypto transfers where Travel Rule applies. Review: Check whether counterparty is a CASP/VASP or self-hosted wallet and whether required information was collected.

Trigger: Transaction involves an unlicensed, non-cooperative, sanctioned, or high-risk crypto service provider. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat and fiat-to-crypto wallet screening. Review: Check counterparty status, jurisdiction, licensing, and blockchain analytics label.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Transaction involves an unlicensed, non-cooperative, sanctioned, or high-risk crypto service provider. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat and fiat-to-crypto wallet screening. Review: Check counterparty status, jurisdiction, licensing, and blockchain analytics label.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Transaction involves an unlicensed, non-cooperative, sanctioned, or high-risk crypto service provider. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat and fiat-to-crypto wallet screening. Review: Check counterparty status, jurisdiction, licensing, and blockchain analytics label.

summary: Trigger: Fiat payment is reversed, recalled, disputed, or charged back after crypto was sent to the requested wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check fraud indicators, customer history, destination wallet, and linked accounts.

Trigger: Customer has multiple failed cards, rejected bank transfers, name mismatches, or payment attempts. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check stolen card risk, mule activity, payment method ownership, and device/IP behavior.

Trigger: Inactive customer suddenly performs high-value activity or uses a new bank account, new wallet, new device, or new IP country. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Check account takeover risk and whether the activity fits prior customer behavior.

Trigger: Customer triggers multiple AML rules within a short period. Applies to: All transactions. Example: Three or more alerts within 30 days. Review: Assess the full pattern, not only each transaction separately.

Trigger: Customer cannot provide reasonable source of funds/source of wealth for transaction size or pattern. Applies to: High-value or unusual fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat transactions. Review: Request documents such as salary evidence, bank statement, sale agreement, business revenue proof, or crypto acquisition proof.
Raises an activity warning when one fiat transaction crosses the client review threshold.
Fixed platform threshold for large individual transaction review.
Raises a one-time warning when accumulated client volume crosses the lower review threshold.
Raises a one-time warning when accumulated client volume crosses the high review threshold.
Raises a warning when a client creates three transactions inside a 24 hour window.

kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer requests crypto to be sent to, or sends crypto from, a wallet linked to mixers, tumblers, coinjoin services, peeling chains, or other obfuscation methods. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Identify whether exposure is direct or indirect and whether the customer has repeated similar activity.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer requests crypto to be sent to, or sends crypto from, a wallet linked to mixers, tumblers, coinjoin services, peeling chains, or other obfuscation methods. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Identify whether exposure is direct or indirect and whether the customer has repeated similar activity.

summary: Trigger: Fiat payment comes from a bank account, card, or payment method not held in the verified customer's name. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Compare sender name with KYC name. Request proof of relationship or reject if third-party funding is prohibited.

Trigger: Customer requests fiat payout to a bank account not held in their own verified name. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check beneficiary ownership, account name, country, and whether payout violates internal policy.

Trigger: Two or more unrelated customers use the same IBAN, card, payment account, or fiat sender/beneficiary details. Applies to: Fiat deposits and payouts. Review: Check shared address, device, IP, wallet address, and possible mule network activity.

Trigger: Multiple unrelated customers request crypto to the same wallet address or send crypto from the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check customer links, common fiat accounts, shared IP/device, and wallet cluster.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Multiple unrelated customers request crypto to the same wallet address or send crypto from the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check customer links, common fiat accounts, shared IP/device, and wallet cluster.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Multiple unrelated customers request crypto to the same wallet address or send crypto from the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check customer links, common fiat accounts, shared IP/device, and wallet cluster.

summary: Trigger: A customer uses several fiat payment methods but repeatedly requests crypto delivery to the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check ownership of all payment methods and whether the wallet appears to collect funds from multiple people.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: A customer uses several fiat payment methods but repeatedly requests crypto delivery to the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check ownership of all payment methods and whether the wallet appears to collect funds from multiple people.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: A customer uses several fiat payment methods but repeatedly requests crypto delivery to the same wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check ownership of all payment methods and whether the wallet appears to collect funds from multiple people.

summary: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from several unrelated wallets and requests fiat payout to the same bank account. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check wallet ownership, source of funds, and whether the customer is acting for third parties.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from several unrelated wallets and requests fiat payout to the same bank account. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check wallet ownership, source of funds, and whether the customer is acting for third parties.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from several unrelated wallets and requests fiat payout to the same bank account. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Check wallet ownership, source of funds, and whether the customer is acting for third parties.

summary: Trigger: Customer performs several transactions just below internal review thresholds. Applies to: Fiat deposits, fiat payouts, crypto purchases, and crypto sales. Example: Three or more transactions just below EUR 1,000 / EUR 3,000 / EUR 10,000 within 24 hours or 7 days. Review: Check whether the pattern appears designed to avoid checks.

Trigger: Newly onboarded customer performs a high-value transaction shortly after registration. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Example: Transaction above EUR 5,000 within 7 days of onboarding. Review: Check KYC quality, source of funds, wallet risk, payment ownership, and IP/device risk.

Trigger: Transaction amount or frequency is inconsistent with customer occupation, income, expected activity, or previous behavior. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Request source of funds/source of wealth where needed.

Trigger: Customer performs unusually frequent transactions in a short period. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat. Example: More than 5 transactions in 24 hours or 10 transactions in 7 days, depending on normal customer behavior. Review: Check whether activity is normal market behavior, business activity, or possible layering.

Trigger: Customer repeatedly changes requested crypto payout wallet addresses. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check whether wallets are connected, high-risk, newly created, or linked to suspicious clusters.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer repeatedly changes requested crypto payout wallet addresses. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check whether wallets are connected, high-risk, newly created, or linked to suspicious clusters.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer repeatedly changes requested crypto payout wallet addresses. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check whether wallets are connected, high-risk, newly created, or linked to suspicious clusters.

summary: Trigger: Customer, fiat bank, IP address, wallet service, or counterparty is linked to a high-risk or restricted jurisdiction. Applies to: Fiat and crypto transactions. Review: Check country risk, customer residency, payment country, IP location, and sanctions restrictions.

Trigger: Customer initiates transaction using VPN/proxy/TOR or from a country inconsistent with KYC/residency. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Check login history, device changes, travel explanation, and whether the country is restricted.

Trigger: Customer sends crypto from a wallet with high-risk blockchain exposure and requests fiat payout. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Review source wallet exposure, incoming transaction history, asset type, and customer explanation.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from a wallet with high-risk blockchain exposure and requests fiat payout. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Review source wallet exposure, incoming transaction history, asset type, and customer explanation.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer sends crypto from a wallet with high-risk blockchain exposure and requests fiat payout. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat. Review: Review source wallet exposure, incoming transaction history, asset type, and customer explanation.

summary: Trigger: Customer pays fiat and requests crypto delivery to a wallet with high-risk indicators. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: This is the corrected version of the earlier rule. The alert is not because the customer buys crypto, but because the requested destination wallet is risky.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer pays fiat and requests crypto delivery to a wallet with high-risk indicators. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: This is the corrected version of the earlier rule. The alert is not because the customer buys crypto, but because the requested destination wallet is risky.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer pays fiat and requests crypto delivery to a wallet with high-risk indicators. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: This is the corrected version of the earlier rule. The alert is not because the customer buys crypto, but because the requested destination wallet is risky.

summary: Trigger: Customer uses privacy coins or assets/chains with reduced traceability, where supported or detected. Applies to: Crypto transactions. Review: Check customer purpose, jurisdiction, amount, and repeated use.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer uses privacy coins or assets/chains with reduced traceability, where supported or detected. Applies to: Crypto transactions. Review: Check customer purpose, jurisdiction, amount, and repeated use.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer uses privacy coins or assets/chains with reduced traceability, where supported or detected. Applies to: Crypto transactions. Review: Check customer purpose, jurisdiction, amount, and repeated use.

summary: Trigger: Customer activity involves rapid conversion between assets/chains before or after using the exchange. Applies to: Mainly crypto-to-fiat, but also relevant for fiat-to-crypto wallet review. Review: Look for bridge use, rapid swaps, obfuscation patterns, and high-risk end wallets.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer activity involves rapid conversion between assets/chains before or after using the exchange. Applies to: Mainly crypto-to-fiat, but also relevant for fiat-to-crypto wallet review. Review: Look for bridge use, rapid swaps, obfuscation patterns, and high-risk end wallets.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Customer activity involves rapid conversion between assets/chains before or after using the exchange. Applies to: Mainly crypto-to-fiat, but also relevant for fiat-to-crypto wallet review. Review: Look for bridge use, rapid swaps, obfuscation patterns, and high-risk end wallets.

summary: Trigger: Incoming or requested wallet is linked to cross-chain bridges, especially combined with high-risk exposure. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Review source chain, destination chain, bridge risk, and wallet path.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Incoming or requested wallet is linked to cross-chain bridges, especially combined with high-risk exposure. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Review source chain, destination chain, bridge risk, and wallet path.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Incoming or requested wallet is linked to cross-chain bridges, especially combined with high-risk exposure. Applies to: Crypto side of transaction. Review: Review source chain, destination chain, bridge risk, and wallet path.

summary: Trigger: Required originator/beneficiary information is missing, incomplete, inconsistent, or rejected by counterparty CASP/VASP. Applies to: Crypto transfers where Travel Rule applies. Review: Check whether counterparty is a CASP/VASP or self-hosted wallet and whether required information was collected.

Trigger: Transaction involves an unlicensed, non-cooperative, sanctioned, or high-risk crypto service provider. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat and fiat-to-crypto wallet screening. Review: Check counterparty status, jurisdiction, licensing, and blockchain analytics label.
kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Transaction involves an unlicensed, non-cooperative, sanctioned, or high-risk crypto service provider. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat and fiat-to-crypto wallet screening. Review: Check counterparty status, jurisdiction, licensing, and blockchain analytics label.

wallet.kycaidRiskReason: Trigger: Transaction involves an unlicensed, non-cooperative, sanctioned, or high-risk crypto service provider. Applies to: Crypto-to-fiat and fiat-to-crypto wallet screening. Review: Check counterparty status, jurisdiction, licensing, and blockchain analytics label.

summary: Trigger: Fiat payment is reversed, recalled, disputed, or charged back after crypto was sent to the requested wallet. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check fraud indicators, customer history, destination wallet, and linked accounts.

Trigger: Customer has multiple failed cards, rejected bank transfers, name mismatches, or payment attempts. Applies to: Fiat-to-crypto. Review: Check stolen card risk, mule activity, payment method ownership, and device/IP behavior.

Trigger: Inactive customer suddenly performs high-value activity or uses a new bank account, new wallet, new device, or new IP country. Applies to: All transactions. Review: Check account takeover risk and whether the activity fits prior customer behavior.
